few_shot_examples.cq: Which scholarly topics can be compared with places that have both enslaved-person and hazard records?
Which publication authors can be connected to historical places that also have hazard events?
Which publications align with enslaved-person records and hazards after filtering by shared place and period?
Which departments publish work connected to historical locations with both demographic records and hazard impact data?
Which authors, departments, fields, publications, enslaved-person records, owners, relationships, events, hazard types, places, periods, damage, and impact details are connected?

reasoning_steps: First, identify scholarly, enslaved-person historical, and hazard-event requirements.
Next, map publications, authors, departments, universities, and fields of study to Core Scholar Rich.
Then, map enslaved-person records to the enslaved KG and hazards, places, temporal scopes, damage, impact, and climate details to KWG.
Finally, select kwg, enslaved, and core_scholar_rich when all three domains are required.

reasoning_example.cq: Which KGs are needed to connect scholarly publications with places that have both enslaved-person records and hazard records?
reasoning: First, I identify that the CQ asks to connect scholarly publications with places that have both enslaved-person records and hazard records.

Core Scholar Rich is needed for publications, authors, departments, universities, fields of study, and publication dates. The enslaved KG is needed for enslaved-person records, owners, relationships, events, places, and temporal extents. KWG is needed for hazards, hazard types, affected places, temporal scopes, damage, impact, and climate details.
No single KG or two-KG pair covers all three required layers: scholarship, historical enslaved-person records, and hazard-event records.
The bridge axes are place, time, historical topic, and scholarly field, so the minimal combination is kwg, enslaved, and core_scholar_rich.
